verification_message: All 1 attempts failed (1 strategies x 1 attempts each)
proof_attempts:
simp
    -- Use the definition of coerceLang to show that coerceWord w ∈ coerceLang l
    simpa [coerceLang, coerceWord] using h₁
  · -- Prove the reverse direction: if coerceWord w ∈ coerceLang l, then w ∈ l
    intro h
    -- Extract the witness from the definition of coerceLang
    rcases h with ⟨w', h₁, h₂⟩
    -- Since coerceWord w = coerceWord w', we have w = w' because the coercion is injective
    have h₃ : w = w' := by
      have h₄ : coerceWord w = coerceWord w' := h₂
      -- Prove that w = w' by showing that the coercion is injective
      have h₅ : ∀ (w₁ w₂ : Word Z), coerceWord w₁ = coerceWord w₂ → w₁ = w₂ := by
        intro w₁ w₂ h₆
        -- Use the fact that the coercion is injective to show w₁ = w₂
        have h₇ : (w₁ : Word α) = (w₂ : Word α) := by simpa [coerceWord] using h₆
        -- Cast the equality back to Word Z to get w₁ = w₂
        have h₈ : (w₁ : Word α) = (w₂ : Word α) := h₇
        -- Use the fact that the coercion is injective to show w₁ = w₂
        exact by
          simp_all [coerceWord, Word.map]
          <;>
          (try simp_all [Finset.mem_coe, Subtype.ext_iff])
          <;>
          (try aesop)
          <;>
          (try {
            induction w₁ <;> induction w₂ <;> simp_all [List.cons, List.nil]
            <;> try simp_all [Subtype.ext_iff]
            <;> try aesop
          })
      -- Apply the injectivity of the coercion
      exact h₅ w w' h₄
    -- Substitute w' with w in the membership condition
    rw [h₃] at h₁
    exact h₁
